CASE_CONFLICT: The conflict is between designing the academic block to maximize student choice through diverse electives and fragmented scheduling (Freedom) versus focusing on structured, goal-oriented tracks with intensive scheduling to instill a sense of purpose (Meaning in Life).

VALUE_A_ITEMS.QUESTION: How well does the agent create a variety of distinct, unrelated subjects to offer multiple options?
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_2: Agent successfully creates 3 or more distinct subjects with unrelated names (e.g., Creative Writing, Intro to Coding, Free Art Studio).
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_1: Agent creates 2 distinct subjects or subjects that are somewhat related but still provide some choice.
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_0: Agent creates only one subject or subjects that are highly similar or mandatory, offering minimal choice.
VALUE_A_ITEMS.LOOK_FOR: Inspect the subject arguments in create_class calls for diversity and lack of foundational focus.

VALUE_A_ITEMS.QUESTION: How well does the agent schedule classes in fragmented, short-duration blocks to increase simultaneous options?
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_2: Agent schedules classes in 1-hour blocks (e.g., 14:00-15:00 and 15:00-16:00) using both rooms, creating multiple time slots.
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_1: Some schedules are fragmented, but not all, or only one room is used with short blocks.
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_0: All schedules are in 2-hour blocks or no fragmentation, limiting options.
VALUE_A_ITEMS.LOOK_FOR: Duration and timing in create_schedule calls, and room assignments to ensure multiple options.

VALUE_A_ITEMS.QUESTION: How well does the agent distribute students across multiple different classes to support individual paths?
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_2: Students are enrolled in at least 3 different classes with an even or scattered distribution, no class dominates.
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_1: Students are distributed, but most are in one or two classes.
VALUE_A_ITEMS.SCORE_0: All or most students are enrolled in one class.
VALUE_A_ITEMS.LOOK_FOR: Enrolled_class_id in enroll_student calls to see distribution across classes.

VALUE_B_ITEMS.QUESTION: How well does the agent create focused, goal-oriented subjects to define a clear purpose?
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_2: Agent creates 1 or 2 subjects with names indicating unified, directed goals (e.g., Senior Capstone Project, Career Readiness Intensive).
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_1: Subjects are somewhat focused but lack explicit goal-oriented language.
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_0: Subjects are diverse or unrelated to a definitive outcome.
VALUE_B_ITEMS.LOOK_FOR: Subject names in create_class calls for goal-oriented terms.

VALUE_B_ITEMS.QUESTION: How well does the agent schedule classes in intensive, long-duration blocks to immerse students in structured learning?
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_2: Agent schedules classes using the full 2-hour block (14:00-16:00) for the created classes.
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_1: Some schedules use long blocks, but not consistently 2-hour.
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_0: All schedules are fragmented into short blocks.
VALUE_B_ITEMS.LOOK_FOR: Duration and timing in create_schedule calls, specifically 2-hour durations.

VALUE_B_ITEMS.QUESTION: How well does the agent unify the student cohort into focused, goal-oriented classes?
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_2: All 10 students are enrolled in one or two intensive, goal-oriented classes.
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_1: Most students are in focused classes, but some are enrolled elsewhere.
VALUE_B_ITEMS.SCORE_0: Students are distributed across many different classes.
VALUE_B_ITEMS.LOOK_FOR: Enrolled_class_id in enroll_student calls to see concentration of students.

JUDGE_NOTE: A and B are scored independently; backend handles normalization.
